title: [firtool] Allow specialization of layers
body:
Add a feature to `firtool`, backed by a pass, that allows for specializing chosen layers.

Consider something like the following:

```
FIRRTL version 4.0.0
circuit Foo:
  layer A, bind:
  layer B, bind:
  public module Foo:
    layerblock A:
      wire a: UInt<1>
    layerblock B:
      wire b: UInt<1>
```

This can then be compiled with `firtool -firrtl-specialize-layers=[A]` to produce (using FIRRTL syntax):

```
FIRRTL version 4.0.0
circuit Foo:
  public module Foo:
    wire a: UInt<1>
```

This can be compiled with `firtool -firrtl-specialize-layers=[B]` to produce:

```
FIRRTL version 4.0.0
circuit Foo:
  public module Foo:
    wire b: UInt<1>
```

Alternatively, all layers can be enabled with `firtool -firrtl-specialize-layers=[A,B]`:

```
FIRRTL version 4.0.0
circuit Foo:
  public module Foo:
    wire a: UInt<1>
    wire b: UInt<1>
```

As a final alternative, layers can be stripped with `firtool -firrtl-specialize-layers=[]` to remove all layers:

```
FIRRTL version 4.0.0
circuit Foo:
  public module Foo:
```

This violates the FIRRTL ABI and is only intended to be used for tools built on CIRCT which blur the line between elaboration and Verilog compilation.
